pattern: Score each report by +3 per positive word and -1 per negative word encountered, then rank students by score descending with ties broken by the smaller student id.
hints: Turn the positive and negative word lists into two sets for O(1) membership checks, then tally each report word by word.
A word counts every time it appears in a sentence, so a repeated positive or negative word affects the score more than once.
Sort students by (-score, id) so higher scores come first and, among equal scores, the smaller id wins -- then take the first k ids.

edge_inputs.description: matches the statement example where a tie is broken by the smaller id
matches the statement example where a negative word lowers one student's score
a positive word repeated twice in one sentence counts twice
input: team was awesome and great and great
quiet class with no comment words

edge_inputs.description: a tie in score must break by the smaller student id, not by input order
k equal to the number of students returns everyone ranked
k = 1 returns only the single top scorer
a single student with a single neutral word

stress.description: random reports built from a fixed positive/negative/neutral vocabulary with shuffled unique ids
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    positive_feedback = ['good', 'great', 'super', 'excellent', 'awesome']
    negative_feedback = ['bad', 'terrible', 'awful', 'poor', 'weak']
    neutral = ['the', 'student', 'is', 'was', 'very', 'quite', 'team', 'project', 'class', 'effort']
    vocab = positive_feedback + negative_feedback + neutral
    report = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(3, 8)
        words = [rng.choice(vocab) for _ in range(length)]
        report.append(' '.join(words))
    ids = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(ids)
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [positive_feedback, negative_feedback, report, ids, k]
